seoTitle: St George vs St Helens Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between St George and St Helens Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: St George vs St Helens Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St George and St Helens Park.
heading: St George vs St Helens Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St George, New South Wales and St Helens Park, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St George has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to St Helens Park (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St George or St Helens Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St George has a flood risk score of 54/100, while St Helens Park scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St George scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. St Helens Park scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St George or St Helens Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St George has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
